candidates.target: MPI ping-pong exchange loop dominated by blocking MPI_Send/MPI_Recv waits
candidates.rationale: Profiling shows MPI_Waitall at 40% and telemetry indicates communication latency dominates; among allowed patterns, Non-Blocking I/O best matches reducing time spent blocked/waiting by converting blocking operations to non-blocking equivalents while preserving message ordering semantics.
candidates.action_sketch: Replace the blocking send/recv pair each iteration with non-blocking MPI_Isend/MPI_Irecv posted in the same per-rank order, then complete with MPI_Waitall (or two MPI_Wait) before reusing/overwriting buf; keep the same rank/tag/communicator and iteration ordering, and ensure the receive completes before checksum/use of buf.
preconditions: MPI implementation supports MPI_Isend/MPI_Irecv and MPI_Waitall correctly
No code path reads/writes the receive buffer before the corresponding wait completes
Per-iteration ordering (recv-before-send on rank 1; send-before-recv on rank 0) is preserved to avoid deadlock/semantic changes
correctness_checks: Verify CHECKSUM matches baseline for multiple msg_size/iters values
Run with MPI message matching checks (same tag=123, ranks, communicator) and ensure no hangs
Validate that buffer is not modified between Isend/Irecv posting and completion
rollback_criteria: TIME_SEC does not improve (or regresses) beyond run-to-run noise versus blocking baseline
Any mismatch in CHECKSUM or occurrence of hang/deadlock
